chemistry.reaction_name: Flow photochemical isomerization of cis-cycloalkenes to trans-cycloalkenes
chemistry.scope_summary: Method applicable to various functionalized cis-cyclooctenes and cis-cycloheptenes, including those with hydroxyl, carbamate, cyano, aryl, and NHS ester groups. Flow chemistry essential for preparative yields as batch conditions give low yields due to photodegradation.

optimization_table.key_findings: Flow photochemical methods provide higher yields for trans-cycloalkene synthesis compared to batch conditions

figures.caption: Figure 1.  Tetrazine ligation with  trans- cycloalkenes has become a broadly used tool for chemical biology, medicine and materials science.
Figure 1.  Tetrazine ligation with  trans- cycloalkenes has become a broadly used tool for chemical biology, medicine and materials science.
Figure 2.  Enantioselective photoisomerization based on chiral ex- ciplex formation.

figures.flow_path: metering pump → reaction vessel → silver capture column
figures.key_information: Photochemical reaction setup with AgNO3 adsorbed on silica or SO3- Ag+ for silver capture, PhCO2Me sensitizer, continuous flow at 100 mL/min
figures.caption: Figure 3.  Schematic of Apparatus for  trans -Cyclooctene Synthesis.
Figure 4.  Examples of  trans- cyclooctene derivatives that have been prepared using flow photoisomerization.

figures.flow_path: AgNO3·SiO2 → metering pump → optically transparent FEP tubing → collection flask
figures.key_information: Photochemical reactor with thermally labile compound, AgNO3 captured as stable Ag-complex, N2 atmosphere
figures.caption: Figure 15.  Flow photoisomerization apparatus for carbocyclic  trans - cycloheptene derivatives uses FEP tubing and inline cooling.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Batch photochemical conditions give very low yields (5%) due to photodegradation over long reaction times (18 hours)
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Flow chemistry enables preparative yields (77%) by minimizing photodegradation through continuous removal of product via AgNO3 complexation
translation_logic.what_changed: Continuous flow setup with inline AgNO3 complexation allows for selective removal of trans-product, preventing photodegradation
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same photosensitizer (methyl benzoate), same light source (254 nm), same general reaction conditions
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow allows for better control of photochemical reactions and handling of thermally labile intermediates

figure_descriptions: Flow photochemical reactor setup with AgNO3 capture column
FEP tubing reactor with inline cooling for thermally labile substrates
Scope table showing various trans-cycloalkene products with yields 51-77%

conflicts.resolution: Used figure value (100) as more specific to reactor scheme

notes: This is a review paper covering flow photochemical methods for trans-cycloalkene synthesis. The dramatic yield improvement (5% to 77%) is attributed to continuous product removal via AgNO3 complexation in flow, preventing photodegradation that occurs in batch. Multiple reactor designs are shown including standard quartz flask and FEP tubing for temperature-sensitive substrates.
